summary.intent: Run parallel code reviews on two sprint stories (1-2 Design Token System and 1-4 Empty Dashboard Cards) using the bmad-code-review workflow, then resolve all findings.
summary.outcome: Both stories were reviewed, a token format decision was made, and all 4 patches were applied across the codebase. Both stories advanced to `done` status.

repo: Sprint story status is tracked in `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml` and individual story markdown files under the same directory.
The bmad-code-review skill runs three review layers: Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, and Acceptance Auditor.
The `--color-accent-glow` token was changed from `rgba(0, 245, 255, 0.15)` to `#00f5ff` so Tailwind v4 opacity modifiers work; call sites should use `bg-accent-glow/15`.

code.finding: Defining a color token with baked-in alpha (rgba) inside @theme prevents Tailwind v4 from generating opacity modifier variants. Use a base color instead and apply opacity at call sites.

`@apply leading-none` in `.price-text` was a no-op because an explicit `line-height: 1.1` immediately followed it. Removed. Also missing responsive font-size: spec requires 36px mobile / 48px desktop.

`<h1 class="sr-only">` placed inside `<main class="grid">` became a grid child, occupying the first 1.5fr column and pushing PriceCard/MarketCapCard out of their intended layout. Moved outside the grid.
Redundant `sm:min-h-[53px]` class on `<output>` element was removed.

workflow: Parallel agent execution for independent code reviews is effective — both agents completed in ~95-110 seconds each with no conflicts.
The bmad-code-review workflow classifies findings into: patch (apply now), decision-needed (requires human input), deferred (out of scope), and dismissed.
Decisions should be resolved before patches are applied to ensure consistent intent across related changes.

friction: After the review agents completed, applying patches required manually re-reading files and re-grepping for symbols, duplicating work the agents had already done.
Updating story statuses and sprint-status.yaml required multiple sequential edits and reads due to needing to verify current file state before each edit.
open_items: Story 1-4: Hardcoded 'Live' status text in static shell — deferred to Story 4.3 when live data wiring is implemented.
Story 1-4: `sm:py-12` over-padding on body — pre-existing artifact from Story 1.3, not addressed.
Story 2-1 (Nano Stores and Number Formatting Utilities) is the next ready-for-dev story and was not started.
